stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
The content points to the ongoing community interest and evaluation of major large language models.

stories.title: Waymo suspends freeway driving amid safety concerns
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Waymo has suspended freeway driving services across all US markets.
The suspension was implemented due to ongoing safety concerns.
Customers noticed the change when their trip projections shifted to longer routes via local roads.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core area of text generation within the AI/ML ecosystem.
The associated tags indicate its relevance to transformers, safetensors, and the Llama family of models.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance on Apple Silicon.
This integration leverages MLX, Apple's native machine learning framework, to speed up LLM inference.
The feature is currently available in a preview release, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core feature emphasizes running AI entirely on the user's own device for privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tools to the open-source community.

stories.title: Google’s AI search is so broken it can ‘disregard’ what you’re looking for
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Google's new AI Overviews feature is experiencing significant functional issues.
The AI is demonstrating poor comprehension, exemplified by a search for 'disregard' yielding an irrelevant chatbot-like response.
This highlights current instability and reliability challenges in large-scale generative AI implementations.

stories.title: Google appeals search monopoly ruling, says it won business ‘fair and square’
stories.author: Lauren Feiner
bullet_points: Google is appealing a federal ruling that declared it an illegal search monopolist.
Google's legal defense asserts that its market success was achieved 'fair and square' through competitive means.
The appeal challenges the ruling, claiming the decision bypassed established legal standards.

stories.title: Four Russian satellites are now within striking distance of an ICEYE radarsat
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: Russian satellites are reportedly close enough to target an ICEYE radarsat.
The capability described is noted as unusual for standard satellite missions.
The story touches upon geopolitical and advanced space technology dynamics.

stories.title: MOSS: Self-Evolution through Source-Level Rewriting in Autonomous Agent Systems
stories.author: Yike Guo
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of current autonomous agents being static post-deployment.
MOSS proposes self-evolution by allowing source-level rewriting, moving beyond merely modifying text artifacts like prompts or skill files.
This aims to allow agents to evolve core logic (routing, hook ordering, state invariants) directly within the code harness.

stories.title: Gated DeltaNet-2: Decoupling Erase and Write in Linear Attention
stories.author: Jan Kautz
bullet_points: Introduces Gated DeltaNet-2, an advancement in linear attention designed to decouple erase and write operations.
Addresses the challenge of editing compressed memory states in recurrent attention models without losing existing associations.
Improves efficiency by reducing sequence mixing to linear time and decoding to constant memory, building upon linear attention concepts.

stories.title: DeltaBox: Scaling Stateful AI Agents with Millisecond-Level Sandbox Checkpoint/Rollback
stories.author: Haibo Chen
bullet_points: Introduces DeltaBox, a system designed to scale stateful AI agents by enabling fast checkpointing and rollback.
Addresses a critical bottleneck in LLM agent development: the high latency (hundreds of milliseconds to seconds) associated with duplicating the entire sandbox state for state exploration.
Achieves millisecond-level C/R by optimizing state management, which is crucial for deep search and large-scale reinforcement learning in AI agents.

stories.title: MOSS: Self-Evolution through Source-Level Rewriting in Autonomous Agent Systems
stories.author: Yike Guo
bullet_points: Addresses a critical limitation in current autonomous agent systems: their static nature post-deployment.
Proposes 'MOSS,' a method for self-evolution that goes beyond merely rewriting text artifacts (like prompts or skill files).
Focuses on evolving the core agent harness—including routing, hook ordering, and state invariants—which is currently untouched.

stories.title: Evaluating Commercial AI Chatbots as News Intermediaries
stories.author: James Zou
bullet_points: Systematic evaluation of six major commercial AI chatbots (Gemini, Grok, Claude, GPT) regarding their news accuracy.
The study tests how these chatbots handle emerging facts across multiple languages and regions using real-time BBC News data.
Highlights the critical, yet unmeasured, role of proprietary search and retrieval-synthesis pipelines in news consumption.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes common ML frameworks and formats such as transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It indicates the use of standard NLP/ML pipelines, specifically 'text-generation'.
The associated tags point to core technologies like transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is expanding its capabilities to support local image generation.
The feature is currently available for macOS users in an experimental capacity.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code functionality within open-source model environments.
It bridges the gap between proprietary models (Anthropic) and local, open-source deployment tools (Ollama).

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Meta’s Forum is part Reddit, part Facebook, and part Google AI Overview
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Meta is launching a new 'Forum' app to house Facebook Groups in a dedicated space.
The app integrates a dedicated AI chatbot, aiming to offer an AI-enhanced community experience.
This move signals Meta's effort to modernize its community features, referencing past iterations like the defunct Groups app.

stories.title: US scrambles to stop Internet users re-creating dead pilots’ voices
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: The story concerns the use of AI/ML to recreate the voices of deceased pilots.
This technology is being used by internet users, raising ethical and legal concerns.
The practice reportedly circumvents existing regulations regarding the disclosure of cockpit audio recordings by the NTSB.

stories.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
stories.author: Pulkit Agrawal
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of current LLMs in handling diverse tasks required by inference-scaling search procedures (like AlphaEvolve).
Standard LLM post-training optimizes for a single scalar reward, which often results in low-entropy, non-diverse outputs.
The proposed 'Vector Policy Optimization' aims to improve LLM generalization and diversity to better support complex, search-based AI systems.

stories.title: The Matching Principle: A Geometric Theory of Loss Functions for Nuisance-Robust Representation Learning
stories.author: Vishal Rajput
bullet_points: Proposes a unified 'Matching Principle' to address various robustness challenges in representation learning.
The core idea is to estimate the covariance of 'label-preserving deployment nuisance' and regularize the encoder Jacobian based on it.
This framework aims to unify disparate problems like domain adaptation, occlusion invariance, and compositional generalization under a single statistical model.

stories.title: LCGuard: Latent Communication Guard for Safe KV Sharing in Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Karthikeyan Natesan Ramamurthy
bullet_points: The paper introduces LCGuard, a mechanism designed to ensure safe sharing of latent communication within multi-agent LLM systems.
It addresses the growing trend of using latent communication (like transformer KV caches) for efficient and information-rich coordination in LLM agents.
The core problem addressed is the opacity and potential security risks associated with KV caches encoding sensitive agent-specific and contextual information.

stories.title: MambaGaze: Bidirectional Mamba with Explicit Missing Data Modeling for Cognitive Load Assessment from Eye-Gaze Tracking Data
stories.author: John Quarles
bullet_points: MambaGaze is a new framework designed for real-time cognitive load assessment using eye-gaze tracking data.
It specifically addresses two major challenges in eye-tracking data: handling missing data (like blinks) and modeling long-range temporal dependencies.
The technology has high potential for safety-critical, human-centered AI applications, such as driver vigilance monitoring.

stories.title: CogAdapt: Transferring Clinical ECG Foundation Models to Wearable Cognitive Load Assessment via Lead Adaptation
stories.author: John Quarles
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of applying large clinical ECG foundation models to real-world, resource-constrained wearable devices.
Proposes CogAdapt, a framework designed to adapt pre-trained clinical ECG models for cognitive load assessment from wearable sensors.
Tackles key limitations in the field: limited labeled data and poor cross-subject generalization for cognitive load monitoring.

stories.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
stories.author: Pulkit Agrawal
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of standard LLM training, which optimizes for a single scalar reward, leading to low-entropy responses.
Proposes 'Vector Policy Optimization' to enable LLMs to generalize and handle diverse reward functions during inference-time search (e.g., AlphaEvolve).
Focuses on improving the diversity of LLM outputs, which is crucial for complex, search-based AI applications.

stories.title: The Matching Principle: A Geometric Theory of Loss Functions for Nuisance-Robust Representation Learning
stories.author: Vishal Rajput
bullet_points: Proposes a unified 'Matching Principle' to address various robustness challenges in representation learning.
The core idea is to estimate the covariance of 'label-preserving deployment nuisance' and regularize the encoder Jacobian based on it.
This framework aims to unify disparate problems like domain adaptation, photometric invariance, and temporal robustness under a single statistical model.

stories.title: LCGuard: Latent Communication Guard for Safe KV Sharing in Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Karthikeyan Natesan Ramamurthy
bullet_points: The research addresses the security/privacy risks associated with sharing latent information (specifically KV caches) in multi-agent LLM systems.
It proposes 'LCGuard,' a mechanism designed to guard or sanitize the information contained within transformer KV caches during inter-agent communication.
The work focuses on improving the efficiency and information richness of multi-agent coordination while mitigating the risks of opaque, sensitive data leakage.

stories.title: FAME: Failure-Aware Mixture-of-Experts for Message-Level Log Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Alberto Leon-Garcia
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of existing log anomaly detectors, which operate at coarse session/window levels.
Proposes 'FAME,' a Failure-Aware Mixture-of-Experts model for fine-grained, message-level log anomaly detection.
Tackles challenges in message-level detection, such as template ambiguity and heterogeneous subsystem failures.

stories.title: Reducing Political Manipulation with Consistency Training
stories.author: Dan Hendrycks
bullet_points: LLMs demonstrate systematic and asymmetric political bias across different sensitive contexts, termed 'covert political bias'.
The research identifies seven distinct categories of techniques through which this covert political bias manifests in LLMs.
Two new metrics, Sentiment Consistency and Helpfulness Consistency, are proposed to quantitatively measure this bias.

stories.title: Understanding Data Temporality Impact on Large Language Models Pre-training
stories.author: Grave Edouard
bullet_points: Investigates how data ordering during pre-training affects Large Language Models' ability to handle time-sensitive factual knowledge.
Addresses the current limitation where LLMs are trained on shuffled corpora, leading to poor temporal grounding.
Introduces a new benchmark comprising over 7,000 temporally grounded questions for evaluation.

stories.title: ChronoMedKG: A Temporally-Grounded Biomedical Knowledge Graph and Benchmark for Clinical Reasoning
stories.author: Richard Röttger
bullet_points: ChronoMedKG addresses a limitation in existing biomedical knowledge graphs (KGs) by incorporating temporal information.
The research highlights that static disease associations fail to capture crucial longitudinal clinical reasoning (e.g., age-dependent symptom relevance).
The introduction of ChronoMedKG provides a new benchmark for improving clinical reasoning and retrieval augmentation in healthcare AI.

stories.title: AnyMo: Geometry-Aware Setup-Agnostic Modeling of Human Motion in the Wild
stories.author: Flora Salim
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of setup dependence in human motion sensing using wearable Inertial Measurement Units (IMUs).
The proposed method, AnyMo, aims to create geometry-aware and setup-agnostic models for human motion in real-world environments.
This advancement is crucial for enabling the broader, cross-device application of wearable IMU data beyond controlled datasets.

stories.title: AMEL: Accumulated Message Effects on LLM Judgments
stories.author: Sid-ali Temkit
bullet_points: The research investigates the 'Accumulated Message Effect on LLM Judgments' (AMEL), examining if prior conversation history biases LLM evaluations.
The study tested 11 LLMs from 4 major providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and open-source models) across nearly 76,000 API calls.
This addresses a critical issue in using LLMs as automated evaluators for tasks like code review and content moderation.

stories.title: A blueprint for formal verification of Apple corecrypto
stories.author: security.apple.com via typesanitizer
bullet_points: The article discusses a blueprint for formally verifying Apple's core cryptography.
Formal verification is a rigorous mathematical method used to prove the correctness of software.
This work is relevant to security and trust in critical systems like Apple's infrastructure.

stories.title: What's Google Antigravity 2.0 ? Here's What the Agent Harness Actually Changes for Developers.
stories.author: om_shree_0709
bullet_points: The article discusses Google's 'Antigravity 2.0' and the implications of the 'Agent Harness' for developers.
It addresses a shift in where AI assistance is being integrated, moving beyond traditional IDE placements.
The content is relevant to developer tooling and the evolution of AI integration in software development.
